races.name: Ronde Van Brugge – Tour of Bruges
races.description: Rebranded for its 50th edition from Classic Brugge-De Panne, this WorldTour sprint classic now loops entirely around Bruges — ditching the treacherous De Panne coastal run-in for a safer city finish on Gulden-Vlieslaan — with the cobbled Brieversweg sector appearing three times as the only real selection point before an expected mass sprint.

broadcasts.notes: Free-to-air on Flemish public broadcaster. VRT Max streams free online.

TV coverage on TNT Sports 2 from 14:15 GMT. Online streaming on Discovery+ (today is the LAST day of Discovery+ streaming — the switch to HBO Max begins tomorrow, March 26, with Stage 4 of the Volta). Subscription ~£30.99/month.
HBO Max Sports Standard plan $18.49/month. This is a Flanders Classic (previously on HBO Max in US as Classic Brugge-De Panne).
Eurosport coverage across Europe. Stream via Discovery+ or HBO Max depending on country.

data_confidence.notes: Start time 13:00 CET / 12:00 GMT confirmed by Domestique how-to-watch guide; finish ~16:30 CET. Distance 202.9km confirmed by PCS and Cyclingnews route page. This race is the rebranded Classic Brugge-De Panne; its PCS URL retains the old slug. Australian broadcaster not confirmed — SBS typically covers ASO/select races; Ronde van Brugge as a Flanders Classic may be on FloBikes AU but is unverified.

races.name: Volta Ciclista a Catalunya – Stage 3
races.description: The last transition stage before three consecutive summit finishes — starting in first-time host Mont-roig del Camp on the Costa Daurada and crossing the Prades mountains (Cat.1 Alt de la Mussara and Cat.2 Coll de Capafonts), before 40 flat kilometres set up an expected reduced bunch sprint in Vila-seca; GC leader Dorian Godon defends a jersey shared on time with Magnus Cort, four seconds ahead of Evenepoel.

broadcasts.notes: FINAL day of Discovery+ streaming for this race — switch to HBO Max begins with Stage 4 (tomorrow, March 26). TNT Sports TV channels unchanged throughout. Discovery+ ~£30.99/month.
ASO race — exclusive to Peacock in the US. Premium plan $10.99/month or $109.99/year.
Free-to-air on Spanish national public broadcaster.

data_confidence.notes: Start time 13:40 CET confirmed by PCS results page and cyclinguptodate preview. Finish ~17:20 CET per cyclinguptodate and idlprocycling previews. GC after Stage 2: 1. Dorian Godon (INEOS) 2. Magnus Cort (UNO-X) s.t. 3. Remco Evenepoel (RBH) +4s 4. Tom Pidcock (PQT) +6s — confirmed by Cyclingnews live feed and official Volta website. Stage 3 favourites reflect mixed sprint/breakaway character; Godon and Cort both listed as able to contest if it finishes in reduced bunch.

races.name: Settimana Internazionale Coppi e Bartali – Stage 1
races.description: The 41st edition opens in entirely new territory — leaving Emilia-Romagna for a five-stage tour of northern Italy — with this symbolic opener weaving through the UNESCO Langhe vineyards from Barbaresco to Barolo, featuring four GPM including the decisive 6.5km La Morra climb at 4.6% with 9.9km to the uphill finish.

broadcasts.notes: No live TV. Delayed highlights broadcast on RaiSport HD from 18:00 CET (17:00 GMT). Live text/stats via tuttobiciweb.it from start.
Free live stream on the Lega Ciclismo Professionistico official YouTube channel. No subscription required.

data_confidence.notes: Start time 11:40–11:42 CET confirmed by multiple Italian sources (oasport, lavocedialba, PCS LiveStats). Finish 15:20–15:50 CET estimated by race organisers. Cyclingnews stages page incorrectly shows this race starting March 24 — all Italian primary sources and PCS LiveStats confirm March 25 start. Broadcast confirmed as highlights-only on RaiSport (no live TV); live YouTube stream confirmed by oasport.it. The YouTube channel URL is an approximation — users should search 'Lega Ciclismo' on YouTube on race day as the exact live stream URL is not published in advance. Startlist quality score is much lower than the Volta/Ronde (2.1 vs WT) — this is largely a development race with six WorldTour teams fielding younger riders.

issues: UK streaming platform transition: Today (March 25) is the LAST day Discovery+ streams cycling in the UK. The switch to HBO Max is confirmed for March 26, starting with Volta a Catalunya Stage 4 and the Women's Ronde van Brugge. The Men's Ronde van Brugge and Volta Stage 3 today are still on Discovery+.
Ronde van Brugge PCS URL retains the old slug 'classic-brugge-de-panne' as the race was only rebranded for 2026; this is the correct verified URL.

No Australian broadcaster confirmed for Ronde van Brugge — this Flanders Classic has historically been on HBO Max/Eurosport in Australia, not SBS. FloBikes is expanding to Australia in 2026 and may carry it.
Coppi e Bartali YouTube stream URL is the channel URL, not a specific live video URL which is only published shortly before broadcast. Users should navigate to the channel on race day.

Cyclingnews stages page continues to list Coppi e Bartali Stage 1 as March 24 — this is confirmed incorrect by all Italian primary sources and PCS LiveStats (official start confirmed 11:42 CET on March 25).
Volta a Catalunya Stage 3 finish time (~17:20 CET) is an estimate; PCS shows no completed speed data yet.
Ronde van Brugge finish time (~16:30 CET) is an estimate from Domestique's broadcast guide.
